requirements.summary: Implement Internet Identity login/logout with session-aware UI that shows the authenticated user and blocks payment actions when signed out.
acceptanceCriteria: User can log in using Internet Identity and log out
UI shows a signed-in state (e.g., user principal) and a signed-out state
All payment actions are disabled or blocked when signed out

file_operations.description: Create a login/logout button using the selected authorization component patterns (Internet Identity via useInternetIdentity) and clear cached data on logout via React Query. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add first-time profile setup (ask for a human-readable name) using authorization component guidance: call getCallerUserProfile and saveCallerUserProfile, and avoid a modal flash by respecting the actor dependency/loading pattern. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create a small auth/session hook that exposes isAuthenticated, principal (string), and current user profile (name) by composing useInternetIdentity + useActor + getCallerUserProfile query; ensure signed-out state returns nulls and signed-in state is stable. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Implement the session-aware top-level UI: header with logo and login button; signed-out landing state; signed-in dashboard + send form; ensure payment UI is not interactive when signed out and that logout clears/refreshes visible data. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Build a signed-in send-payment form with recipient, amount, memo, and clear success/error feedback, refreshing balance and history on success.
acceptanceCriteria: Form validates recipient principal format and numeric amount before submission
On success, UI shows a confirmation including amount and recipient
On failure, UI shows a human-readable error message returned/mapped from the backend
After a successful send, balance and history views refresh automatically

file_operations.description: Add frontend validation helpers for parsing/validating principal text and numeric amount input (including non-empty, > 0, and integer handling for bigint conversion) before submission.

Create a transfer hook that calls the backend capability transferFunds(recipient, amount, memo), maps errors into human-readable messages, and invalidates/refetches balance + transactions queries on success.

Implement the Send payment form UI (recipient principal, amount, optional memo) gated to authenticated users; validate inputs before calling useTransferFunds; render inline success confirmation and backend error messages; disable submit while pending and when signed out.
Wire the SendPaymentForm into the signed-in view and ensure it triggers visible refreshes (via shared React Query keys) so the balance and history update immediately after a successful send.

requirements.summary: Add a signed-in dashboard showing current balance and a sent/received transaction history with clean loading/empty/error states.
acceptanceCriteria: Balance is loaded from the backend and displayed prominently
Transaction history loads from the backend and distinguishes sent vs received
History handles empty state and loading/error states cleanly

file_operations.description: Create a balance query hook calling the backend capability getBalance(), with loading and error state surfaced for the UI.
Create a transactions query hook calling the backend capability listTransactions(), returning a capped list as provided by the backend and exposing loading/empty/error states.
Add a balance display card that formats bigint for display and clearly highlights the user’s current balance, handling loading/error UI states.

Render transaction history with sent vs received visual distinction (based on comparing sender/recipient to the current principal), showing amount, counterparty, timestamp, and memo; include empty state plus loading/error states.
Add an optional demo helper hook calling the backend capability mintForDemo(); on success invalidate/refetch balance and transactions so first-time users can quickly try sending payments.
Compose BalanceCard, optional demo-mint action, and TransactionHistoryList into a cohesive signed-in dashboard section.
Wire the Dashboard into the signed-in UI and ensure it is only visible/usable when authenticated; keep signed-out users on the landing state.

requirements.summary: Create and apply a consistent finance-themed visual design system using a non-blue/non-purple primary palette.
acceptanceCriteria: App has a consistent non-blue/non-purple primary color and a secondary accent
All main UI (header, cards, forms, lists, buttons) follows the chosen theme
Theme is applied consistently across signed-out and signed-in states

file_operations.description: Create global styling with a cohesive payments theme using CSS variables consumed by Tailwind (e.g., primary in emerald/green and a warm accent like amber), plus consistent typography/spacing defaults; ensure no blue/purple primary palette is used.
Adjust/confirm Tailwind theme usage to align with the new CSS variable palette (primary/secondary/accent/background/foreground) so all components inherit the consistent non-blue/non-purple theme.
Apply the theme consistently in layout and shared UI surfaces (header, main container, cards, buttons, forms, lists) for both signed-out and signed-in states.

requirements.summary: Add generated static logo and hero illustration assets and render them in the header and signed-out landing state.
acceptanceCriteria: Images exist in frontend/public/assets/generated with the specified filenames
UI renders the logo in the app header (or equivalent prominent location)
UI renders the hero/background illustration on the signed-out landing state without loading from the backend

file_operations.description: Add the generated logo asset at the exact required path: frontend/public/assets/generated/paymark-logo.dim_512x512.png.
Add the generated hero/background illustration asset at the exact required path: frontend/public/assets/generated/payments-hero.dim_1600x900.png.

Render the logo from frontend/public/assets/generated/paymark-logo.dim_512x512.png in the app header and render the hero/illustration from frontend/public/assets/generated/payments-hero.dim_1600x900.png in the signed-out landing state as static assets (no backend loading).
